day_title: Chat resilience, release guardrails, and cleaner loop controls
day_intro: This nightly tightened a few reliability seams across the product: chat now keeps pending messages and clearer errors visible, release tooling gained stronger changelog validation, and loop controls respect the model the user actually selected.
highlights: Pending chat messages survive refresh and recovery
Release tooling now rejects weak changelog drafts
Loop controls respect the active model selection
Desktop chat surfaces clearer API errors

entries.title: Chat message recovery feels steadier
entries.summary: Early interface work focused on keeping pending messages visible through refreshes and cleaning up duplicate optimistic copies after a stream recovers.

items.text: Pending chat messages are now preserved through refreshes so users do not lose in-flight turns when the page reloads unexpectedly.
Optimistic chat rows are deduplicated once the recovered stream catches up, avoiding a brief double-rendered message state.

entries.title: Release notes now face stricter publication checks
entries.summary: Release infrastructure changes turned changelog generation into a more defensive workflow by retrying stale sessions and screening drafts against a stronger quality bar.

items.text: The release pipeline now retries changelog generation when it detects a stale cached session instead of quietly accepting a broken draft.
Generated changelog drafts are validated against a stricter editorial rubric before they can be published.

entries.title: Manual loop runs follow the chosen model
entries.summary: Loop controls were tightened so manual runs no longer drift back to a default model when a user has already picked something else.

items.text: Manual loop controls now forward the active model selection into execution, keeping loop behavior aligned with the user's current configuration.

entries.title: Desktop chat shows more useful failures
entries.summary: The last desktop-focused fix makes chat failures easier to understand by surfacing clearer API error messages in context.

items.text: Desktop chat now surfaces clearer API failures so users can act on the real error instead of a vague fallback message.
